title: Achnatherum coreanum
taxonomy: Achnatherum coreanum is classified under the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the tribe Stipeae and the genus Achnatherum. This species is also known as Korean needlegrass. Achnatherum coreanum is native to East Asia, including China and the Korean Peninsula. The classification of Achnatherum coreanum reflects its characteristics as a perennial grass.

intro: Achnatherum coreanum, also known as Corean wormwood grass, is a type of plant native to East Asia that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae). This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing fever, alleviating pain, and fighting against infection. The bioactive constituents of Achnatherum coreanum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Achnatherum coreanum is used to make tea, infusions, and decoctions, which are the most common herbal preparations made with this plant, often used to promote health and well-being in traditional medicine.
